videos.title: 9 POWERFUL Things That Happen To Your Body When You Drink Beet Juice

transcript.fullText: Hey there, viewers! Let’s talk about  beetroots. Their pinkish-red color will make anyone fall in love with them. They go well in  salads and soups. But cooking them can destroy their nutrients. Juicing remains the easiest  and healthiest way to eat fresh beetroot. Get ready to have a major beetroot meltdown, as  we discuss the 9 benefits of beet juice in today's video. Does it boost your stamina? Can it help you  lose weight? Let's discuss all this and more... 1. Reduces Blood Pressure There’s a reason beetroots are considered a  superfood. It can lower your blood pressure, and reduce your chances of heart disease. Hypertension is a serious condition across  the globe. It claims so many lives every year. People pop medications, when all they really  need to do is have a glass of beetroot juice. Drinking just 250 ml of juice on the regular can  have a significant effect on your blood pressure. Beetroots have a high level of nitrates. After  having beet juice, the nitrates mix with your blood. In the process, they form a new compound  called nitric oxide. This powerful chemical can widen and relax all those tight blood vessels. As  soon as this happens, blood gushes to important organs like your brain, heart and muscles.  This balances out your blood pressure. What’s your favorite type of homemade  juice? Sound off in the comment section, and start a conversation  with our Bestie community... 2. Increases Stamina Do you ever finish a workout and think, “If  only I lifted a little harder”? Beetroot juice can make this a reality. This is why most  athletes have a fresh glass before a game. This vegetable gives strength to your muscles,  helping you deal with that extra stress. This is again thanks to the nitrates. Let  me break it down more. Oxygen is required for your body to perform its tasks. More  oxygen is better for their functioning. Constricted blood vessels carry less oxygen  to the muscles. Nitrates will increase their oxygen-carrying capacity. With the fuel from  oxygen, your muscles kick into full swing. A study found out that trained cyclists who had just 2 cups of beet juice could  improve their trail by 12 seconds. It's about time you said goodbye to orange,  apple, or any other popular breakfast juice. Beet juice is great for waking  those sleepy organs up. It serves as a great pre-workout to prepare  your body for physical exhaustion. It also helps with weight loss, as it has  almost zero calories. It’s second only to water. Looking for answers on all the latest health  and wellness news? Hit that “subscribe” button, and join our millions of followers. Stay  up to date on all our great Bestie content… 3. Glowing Skin Not all people are blessed with flawless skin. If you constantly hide behind filters on  Instagram, this one's for you. No amount of makeup or skin cream can change your skin’s  texture & quality. You need a boost from within. Beetroot juice is a great blood purifier and will improve circulation. With more supply of blood  to the skin, it will be firm and radiant. There will be visible differences, as those dark  spots, blemishes and dead cells start fading away. Vitamin C in beet juice can give your skin a blast  of antioxidants and keep you looking younger. Say goodbye to make-up as your cheeks  are going to be blushing naturally. 4. Heart Health Beetroot juice is heart-friendly. You  probably think it would take months to see the positive effects  of beet juice on your health. But it happens a lot quicker than that.  Your heart requires the help of surrounding muscles to pump blood. If the muscles  become fatigued, your heart could fail. A study shows that people with heart failure  experienced a 13% rise in muscle power. This happened within just two hours of drinking a glass  of beet juice. Seems like sorcery, but it's true. Before we move ahead, here’s another  video on heart health you should consider. Watch 8 signs your body gives you  a month before a heart attack. Now back to our discussion on 9  Health Benefits of Beet Juice... 5. Beats Dementia Beet juice guarantees a younger and more  active brain. When you start growing old, your body’s unable to produce the same  amount of nitrates it did years ago. Beet juice provides that extra boost. A few studies show that the blood flow to the  frontal lobe increases after consuming beet juice. Different parts of the brain are associated  with different activities your body performs. Frontal lobes are linked to memory,  judgment, impulse control, and cognition. With more blood in this area, it  functions like a well-oiled machine. 6. Fights Cancer A few foods are known to keep cancer at  bay. Beetroots are one of them. The rich color of beetroot is brought to you by betalains  (beta-lains). These are soluble antioxidants. This colored chemical can destroy  unstable cancer cells in your body. Researchers call these chemical compounds  scavengers, as they drag free radicals out of your body and keep you healthy. Now,  more research is required in this field, but from what we have so far, we can safely  say that beets are super effective against breast cancer. They can also prevent  cancer of the lung, colon, and liver. 7. Doubles as Blood Tonic This veggie juice is the coolest  way to remove toxins from the body. It improves circulation and stimulates red  blood cell production. It's the best homemade tonic for people running low on hemoglobin.  It’s also good for people who are anemic. Make it a point to have a glass each  day, especially if you’re pregnant. The folate in beetroots will reduce your  baby's risk of being born prematurely. It can also help prevent certain diseases  from developing along your spine. Beetroot juice can also be great if you're  planning a pregnancy in the future. It will keep you in the best shape possible. If you  don't enjoy its earthy taste, add a few carrots, along with some celery, apples and lemons. These  will provide you with so many extra nutrients. On top of that, you can add ginger and mint.  This will help put an end to indigestion. Blend it all together. If you want to make  it thicker, try not putting in as much water. If you want to make it more  refreshing, add a couple ice cubes. Aside from folate, your body will get  a boost of other essential minerals. These work to strengthen your immune system  and make your bones and teeth rock hard. These minerals include things like iron, magnesium,  manganese, sodium, zinc, copper, and selenium. Finally, beets just aren’t beets without  Potassium. This mineral is so amazing, it serves as a natural cure for sore muscles  and nerves. If you’ve been feeling tired, try having one glass daily to improve  your strength. You won’t regret it. 8. Detoxifies Your Liver Your liver is probably the most hardworking  organ in your body. If you don’t have a healthy, functioning liver, it could mean your  life. When your eating habits are bad, and all you’re stuffing into your body is  junk food, your liver is left miserable. Make a conscious effort to relieve your  liver, and start drinking more beet juice. If you’re not convinced, just  think about this. All those wild party nights drinking and eating are  exposing your liver to toxic substances. Your liver can get gross and fatty. This  doesn’t nothing but harm to your health. 1. Reduces Blood Pressure
There’s a reason beetroots are considered a  superfood. It can lower your blood pressure,
and reduce your chances of heart disease.
Hypertension is a serious condition across  the globe. It claims so many lives every year.

blood. In the process, they form a new compound  called nitric oxide. This powerful chemical can
widen and relax all those tight blood vessels. As  soon as this happens, blood gushes to important
organs like your brain, heart and muscles.  This balances out your blood pressure.

improve circulation. With more supply of blood  to the skin, it will be firm and radiant.

Vitamin C in beet juice can give your skin a blast  of antioxidants and keep you looking younger.
Say goodbye to make-up as your cheeks  are going to be blushing naturally.
4. Heart Health
Beetroot juice is heart-friendly. You  probably think it would take months
to see the positive effects  of beet juice on your health.
But it happens a lot quicker than that.  Your heart requires the help of surrounding
muscles to pump blood. If the muscles  become fatigued, your heart could fail.
A study shows that people with heart failure  experienced a 13% rise in muscle power. This
happened within just two hours of drinking a glass  of beet juice. Seems like sorcery, but it's true.

Watch 8 signs your body gives you  a month before a heart attack.

5. Beats Dementia
Beet juice guarantees a younger and more  active brain. When you start growing old,
your body’s unable to produce the same  amount of nitrates it did years ago.
Beet juice provides that extra boost.
A few studies show that the blood flow to the  frontal lobe increases after consuming beet juice.
Different parts of the brain are associated  with different activities your body performs.
Frontal lobes are linked to memory,  judgment, impulse control, and cognition.
With more blood in this area, it  functions like a well-oiled machine.

A few foods are known to keep cancer at  bay. Beetroots are one of them. The rich
color of beetroot is brought to you by betalains  (beta-lains). These are soluble antioxidants.
This colored chemical can destroy  unstable cancer cells in your body.
Researchers call these chemical compounds  scavengers, as they drag free radicals out
of your body and keep you healthy. Now,  more research is required in this field,
but from what we have so far, we can safely  say that beets are super effective against
breast cancer. They can also prevent  cancer of the lung, colon, and liver.

tonic for people running low on hemoglobin.  It’s also good for people who are anemic.

Your liver is probably the most hardworking  organ in your body. If you don’t have a healthy,
functioning liver, it could mean your  life. When your eating habits are bad,

transcript.fullText:
The health benefits of taking apple cider
vinegar and cayenne pepper, and a recipe to use at home. 1. Cayenne Pepper is a well-known powdered spice
and is used in herbal medicine all over the world. It has a powerful effect on improving digestion
and fighting the common cold. 2. Apple Cider Vinegar has been used for thousands
of years to treat all kinds of diseases and infections. It is most commonly used to help the body
absorb nutrients from foods and get rid of acid reflux. 3. When these two ingredients are mixed together
in the form of a drink, they have the ability to help you lose weight quickly by improving
overall digestive health. Let's take a look at the recipe You Will Need 250ml Water 1 Tablespoon Apple Cider Vinegar
1/2 Teaspoon of Cayenne Pepper Method: Add all of the ingredients to a glass and stir well. Drink this first thing in the morning every
day. 4. This drink quickly begins to boost the metabolism
of the body. The cayenne pepper powder contains compounds
which may make the stomach feel warm. 5. The apple cider vinegar boosts stomach acid
which is needed in order to break down foods more effectively. 6. After taking this mixture for a week, your
body will have improved digestion and will be able to tap into it's stored body fat for
energy. 7. This works well when following any weight
loss plan or diet. We recommend following the ketogenic diet
whilst increasing your intake of leafy green vegetables. 8. The nutrients from the vegetables will be
more easily absorbed and your body will become more healthy. A healthy body has the ability to regulate
it's own weight naturally and fat loss is an added benefit. 9. The apple cider vinegar in the mixture also
works to treat a common condition called acid reflux or GERD. Acid reflux is caused by the stomach being
too alkaline. 10. This drink is also very effective at reducing
joint and bone pain for those suffering with arthritis. It purifies the liver and kidneys and lowers
inflammation. 11. We recommend using high quality products,
such as raw unfiltered apple cider vinegar and a good cayenne pepper powder for the best
effects. 12. Many people who drink this tonic on a daily
basis find that they no longer suffer with colds in the winter. It has a powerful effect on boosting the immune
system. 13. The cayenne pepper may also prevent food poisoning
in certain situations, as it has the ability to destroy harmful pathogens in foods. 14. We love adding a dash of cayenne to curries
and homemade healthy soups to add a little heat and nutrition. 15. Drinking a good quality mineral water throughout
the day will also help to balance your electrolytes during weight loss. Making you feel less fatigued and more energetic. 16. Try to reduce your intake of carbohydrates
to as little as possible for the fastest possible weight loss. Instead increase your fat intake from healthy
sources such as olive oil or avocado. 17. To learn more about apple cider vinegar, or
cayenne pepper powder; please see our other videos. Thank you very much for listening, a like is always appreciated and remember to subscribe
and tap the bell for more healthy videos. I wish you great health, wealth and happiness.

segments.text:
The health benefits of taking apple cider
vinegar and cayenne pepper, and a recipe to
use at home.

Cayenne Pepper is a well-known powdered spice
and is used in herbal medicine all over the
world.

It has a powerful effect on improving digestion
and fighting the common cold.

Apple Cider Vinegar has been used for thousands
of years to treat all kinds of diseases and
infections.

It is most commonly used to help the body
absorb nutrients from foods and get rid of
acid reflux.

When these two ingredients are mixed together
in the form of a drink, they have the ability

to help you lose weight quickly by improving
overall digestive health.
Let's take a look at the recipe
You Will Need
250ml Water

1 Tablespoon Apple Cider Vinegar
1/2 Teaspoon of Cayenne Pepper
Method:
Add all of the ingredients to a glass and
stir well.

Drink this first thing in the morning every
day.

This drink quickly begins to boost the metabolism
of the body.

The cayenne pepper powder contains compounds
which may make the stomach feel warm.

The apple cider vinegar boosts stomach acid
which is needed in order to break down foods
more effectively.

After taking this mixture for a week, your
body will have improved digestion and will

be able to tap into it's stored body fat for
energy.

This works well when following any weight
loss plan or diet.

We recommend following the ketogenic diet
whilst increasing your intake of leafy green
vegetables.

The nutrients from the vegetables will be
more easily absorbed and your body will become
more healthy.

A healthy body has the ability to regulate
it's own weight naturally and fat loss is
an added benefit.

The apple cider vinegar in the mixture also
works to treat a common condition called acid
reflux or GERD.

Acid reflux is caused by the stomach being
too alkaline.

This drink is also very effective at reducing
joint and bone pain for those suffering with
arthritis.

It purifies the liver and kidneys and lowers
inflammation.

We recommend using high quality products,
such as raw unfiltered apple cider vinegar

and a good cayenne pepper powder for the best
effects.

Many people who drink this tonic on a daily
basis find that they no longer suffer with
colds in the winter.

It has a powerful effect on boosting the immune
system.

The cayenne pepper may also prevent food poisoning
in certain situations, as it has the ability
to destroy harmful pathogens in foods.

We love adding a dash of cayenne to curries
and homemade healthy soups to add a little
heat and nutrition.

Drinking a good quality mineral water throughout
the day will also help to balance your electrolytes
during weight loss.
Making you feel less fatigued and more energetic.

Try to reduce your intake of carbohydrates
to as little as possible for the fastest possible
weight loss.

Instead increase your fat intake from healthy
sources such as olive oil or avocado.

To learn more about apple cider vinegar, or
cayenne pepper powder; please see our other
videos.
Thank you very much for listening, a like

is always appreciated and remember to subscribe
and tap the bell for more healthy videos.
I wish you great health, wealth and happiness.

videos.title: Lemon Juice and Olive Oil: Benefits for Health

transcript.fullText: the health benefits of lemon juice and olive oil and a simple recipe to use at home extra virgin olive oil is one of the healthiest oils on the planet and is loaded with antioxidants and healthy fats it has been used for at least 5 000 years for its many health benefits and is a staple in the mediterranean diet lemon juice was also used by many ancient civilizations for purifying the body especially in the middle east it is still used today to strengthen the body against disease and for its wonderful flavor in cooking when these ingredients are mixed together they form an amazing remedy which has some fantastic health benefits these work wonders in cleansing the body of toxins nourishing the body and fighting diseases recipe you will need one half a tablespoon of olive oil and one half a tablespoon of lemon juice method mix the ingredients together in a small jar and shake well drink this once per day before a meal or on an empty stomach every morning before breakfast it is very important that you use extra virgin olive oil as this contains the antioxidants and phytonutrients that the body needs you should also use freshly squeezed raw lemon juice and avoid bottled varieties store-bought lemon juice is often pasteurized and does not contain the important vitamin c this mixture also works well as a salad dressing feel free to add a little stevia or raw honey for sweetness let's take a look at 17 benefits and uses of this healing mixture that you can enjoy when consuming it on a daily basis 1. kidney stones the citrates from the lemon juice help to prevent the formation of kidney stones it does this by helping the kidneys to flush out excess calcium and oxalates which come from plant-based foods olive oil works well in this mixture as it also helps you to absorb and digest minerals from vegetables 2. anti-aging the phenols in olive oil work with the vitamin c in lemon juice to slow the effects of aging on the body these are both packed full of antioxidants which fight oxidative damage to the dna three skin health drinking this mixture daily is wonderful for improving the health of the skin the vitamin c from lemons helps to produce collagen for strong healthy skin whilst the vitamin e from olive oil protects the skin from free radical damage together these can reduce blemishes pimples dark circles and age spots four heals arteries one of the main benefits of this mixture is its ability to help heal the artery walls from damage it is highly anti-inflammatory and can reduce the risk of arterial plaque and blood clots this reduces the risk of heart attacks strokes and embolisms naturally 5. varicose veins if you suffer with varicose veins or spider veins then drinking this daily can help lemon juice has strengthening tightening and anti-inflammatory effects on the vein walls you can also massage this mixture into the skin to help increase circulation and reduce pain 6. dandruff you can also apply this mixture directly to the scalp to fight off dandruff and flaky skin both ingredients are anti-fungal and antimicrobial meaning that they kill off any harmful microbes from the scalp and also help the skin to heal naturally seven absorb nutrients another fantastic benefit to this mixture is that it helps your body to absorb nutrients from other foods that you consume especially vegetables eating a salad with wild caught fish in cruciferous vegetables alongside this combination helps to nourish your cells with vitamins and minerals fast 8. inflammation olive oil contains oleocanthal and other antioxidants that have been shown to reduce inflammation in the body studies show that this is very effective at reducing pain and works similar to ibuprofen lemon juice also lowers inflammation especially when it comes to aching gums tooth pain and aching muscles 9. hair growth a mixture of lemon juice and olive oil is also used by many beauticians to promote hair growth applying this as a hair mask can strengthen the hair follicles which makes them less likely to break and split we also recommend adding a little warmed coconut oil to this to boost its effects 10 helps arthritis the alpha linoleic acid found in virgin olive oil has been linked to reducing arthritis pain and stiffness of the joints this has been shown to reduce inflammation especially for those with rheumatoid arthritis lemon juice helps your body to digest minerals which it can then use to strengthen the joints and connective tissues 11. furniture polish to use this mixture as a furniture polish simply add an extra half a tablespoon of olive oil massage this into wood furniture using a soft cloth following the grain of the wood wipe away excess liquid with a spare cloth be sure to test this in a small area to ensure that you are happy with the results 12. detoxification both of these nutrient-rich foods contain properties which help to detoxify the liver of harmful chemicals the antioxidants phenols and vitamins work hand in hand to cleanse the internal organs and strip away fat from the liver this improves your hormone balance energy levels and general well-being 13. healthy nails carefully apply this mixture to the nailed cuticle to help strengthen the nails helping them to grow stronger and healthier consuming this remedy daily orally will also help to build collagen and absorb more minerals for healthier nail growth 14. gallbladder stones the citric acid in the lemon juice helps to control the flow of bile from the liver and the gallbladder this can naturally reduce the risk of gallbladder stones and protect against inflammation in this organ research shows that people who consume lots of olive oil have a lower incidence of gallstones 15. skin whitening if you have dark patches of skin that you wish to lighten you can use this lemon juice and olive oil mixture to do this simply apply the mixture to the skin and scrub gently using a cotton pad repeat this process every two days until the area has lightened this works by gently exfoliating the skin and restoring its pigment with the citric acid and vitamin c sixteen nutrition this combination contains healthy doses of vitamin c oleic and linoleic acid palmitic acid vitamin k citric acid potassium folate and choline it is also rich in phytonutrients and antioxidants 17. brain health scientific studies show that consuming olive oil regularly can protect the brain against different forms of dementia the antioxidants and essential fatty acids in today's mixture helps to reduce inflammation in the structure of the brain as you can see lemon juice and olive oil is an amazing combination for human health especially when taken on an empty stomach or used as a salad dressing to learn more about natural remedies nutrition and healthy tonics please see our other videos thank you very much for listening a like is always appreciated and remember to subscribe and tap the bell for more healthy videos i wish you great health wealth and happiness

transcript.fullText: Did you know that one of the healthiest fruit juices on the planet can become dangerous with just one wrong ingredient? Many people start their morning feeling proud. They juice a fresh beetroot, pour it into a glass, and believe they are fueling their heart, cleansing the liver, and boosting energy for the entire day. What most people do not realize is that beetroot is extremely powerful. And like anything powerful, it requires respect. Inside this humble red root are compounds that can transform circulation, open blood vessels, support liver function, and increase stamina. However, these same compounds react strongly when combined with the wrong foods. In this video, we will uncover seven beet juice combinations you should never make, including one that can be truly dangerous for anyone over 50 or for those living with kidney or digestive issues. More importantly, I will show you five precise principles that allow your heart, liver, circulation, and energy to benefit safely and effectively. Before we begin, please press the like button, leave a comment to let me know where you are watching from, and subscribe so you never miss important health information designed specifically for men over 50. Now, let me share what you need to know. What you learn today may permanently change how you prepare this healing juice. Every day, thousands of people unknowingly create beet juice combinations that irritate the stomach, trigger fermentation, increase oxalate levels, overload the liver, impair circulation, and even neutralize the very nutrients they are trying to absorb. The most alarming part is that these harmful blends appear healthy on the surface. So, what is really happening inside the body? Mistake number one is combining beet juice with carrots. Beetroot and carrots appear to be a perfect pair. Two brightly colored, nutrient-dense roots that are commonly combined in juices, smoothies, and detox blends. Individually, each vegetable is highly beneficial. But when juiced together in concentrated raw form, they create a silent overload that the kidneys, liver, and blood sugar regulation systems struggle to manage. Both beetroot and carrots are high oxalate vegetables. When consumed separately in moderate portions, oxalates are manageable for most people. But when combined into a powerful juice, oxalate concentration rises sharply. This significantly increases the risk of calcium oxalate kidney stones, especially in older adults, people with diabetes, those who are dehydrated, and anyone with even mild kidney impairment. Oxilates from both roots bind tightly with calcium, forming sharp crystals that accumulate silently until they cause pain, inflammation, or urinary complications. The second issue is blood sugar impact. Both beetroot and carrots contain natural sugars. When juiced, the fiber that slows absorption is removed, allowing sugar to enter the bloodstream rapidly. Without fiber to buffer sugar and oxalates, the juice fermentss quickly in the gut, leading to bloating, gas, and stomach heaviness. Older adults with slower digestion or low stomach acid are particularly vulnerable to these effects. Again, the problem is not the vegetables themselves, but the concentrated combination. Each root is beneficial when eaten whole, cooked, or consumed separately on alternating days. If you are drinking beet juice today, skip the carrots. And if you want carrot juice, enjoy it on its own or combine it with low oxalate, low sugar additions such as cucumber, celery, or a small slice of green apple. In this way, you support kidney function, protect blood sugar balance, and still benefit from the nutritional power of both roots, just not in the same glass. Now, let us move on to how to drink beet juice the right way. Mistake number two is mixing beet juice with honey or sugar. This is one of the most common mistakes people make and unfortunately it is also one of the most harmful. Many people dislike the earthy taste of beetroot. So they try to fix it by adding honey, table sugar or even sweet syrup. It feels harmless. After all, honey is natural, right? But the moment you add concentrated sugar to beet juice, you completely change how the body processes it. Beetroot is naturally rich in nitrates that convert into nitric oxide, a compound that relaxes blood vessels, supports circulation, improves endurance, and helps regulate blood pressure. When beet juice is combined with honey or sugar, this nitrate conversion process is disrupted. As a result, the very benefit most people drink beet juice for, improved blood flow, becomes significantly weaker. What remains is a sugary drink that spikes blood sugar and delivers only a fraction of the cardiovascular support that beetroot can provide on its own. If you truly need to soften the flavor, choose low impact companions such as green apple or cucumber. These add freshness, fiber, and hydration without interfering with nitrates or stressing blood sugar levels. In short, sugar and beet juice do not belong together. If you want beet juice to function as a natural remedy, keep it clean and simple. Your heart, liver, and energy levels will feel the difference. Mistake number three is mixing beet juice with orange. On the surface, this combination sounds healthy. Two vitamin rich fruits blended into a refreshing drink. But for many people, especially older adults or anyone with digestive sensitivity, combining beet juice with orange can quickly backfire. The problem begins with acidity. Oranges are highly acidic and when that acid meets the natural sugars and compounds in beetroot, the result is intestinal fermentation. This leads to excess gas production, bloating, cramping, and uncomfortable abdominal pressure. For individuals with gastritis, acid reflux, or irritable bowel syndrome, this reaction can occur almost immediately. The natural sugars in both beetroot and orange are rapidly absorbed when juiced and no longer buffered by their original fiber structure. They enter the bloodstream quickly. This can cause sharper blood sugar spikes than expected, especially in older adults, people with pre-diabetes, or those with unstable blood pressure. Beet juice works best when the stomach is relatively empty, while orange juice is better tolerated earlier in the day and alongside food to reduce its acidity. So, while the bright color and flavor may appear appealing, beetroot combined with orange causes more harm than benefit when digestive comfort, consistency, and cardiovascular health matter most. If this information is new to you, press the like button and we will move on to the next mistake. Mistake number four is mixing beet juice with vinegar. This may seem like a harmless blend. Vinegar appears in detox drinks, homemade tonics, and countless health tips. But when it comes to beet juice, combining it with vinegar is one of the biggest mistakes you can make. Beetroot is naturally rich in nitrates, compounds that convert into nitric oxide and help open blood vessels, improve circulation, and support healthy blood pressure. Vinegar, however, is extremely acidic, and that acidity can destroy a significant portion of these nitrates before your body has a chance to use them. In other words, you lose the primary benefit that makes beet juice valuable in the first place. The issue goes deeper than nutrient loss. The high acetic acid content in vinegar can irritate the stomach lining when combined with raw beet juice, especially in people who already have acid reflux, gastritis, or sensitive digestion. The best approach is simple. Keep beet juice pure. If you want a lighter, more refreshing texture, use water, cucumber, or low acid fruits such as green apple. Vinegar has its own benefits, just not when mixed with beetroot. Use it in salad dressings or cooking, but keep it away from your morning beet juice if you want to protect your stomach, blood pressure, and the healing compounds that make beetroot such a powerful food. Mistake number five is mixing beet juice with milk or dairy products. Some people try to blend beet juice with milk to make it richer, but this is one of the worst combinations you can create. On the surface, it appears harmless. Beetroot provides nutrients and milk provides protein. However, inside the body, this mixture triggers reactions that lead to digestive disturbance, nutrient loss, and inflammation. The problem begins with acid protein interaction. Beetroot has natural acidity and when beet juice comes into contact with milk proteins, especially casein, it causes them to coagulate prematurely. This coagulation does not occur gently like yogurt fermentation. It happens abruptly inside the stomach. The result is heavy digestion, fermentation, and uncomfortable symptoms such as bloating, gas, reflux, and prolonged stomach discomfort that can last for hours. But the issue goes beyond digestion. Milk requires a very specific digestive environment while beetroot stimulates stomach acid and boflow. When these two processes collide, the body struggles to break down both foods properly. This means you absorb less calcium from the milk and fewer vitamins from the beetroot, which is the opposite of what most people intend when making what they believe is a healthy smoothie. For individuals with lactose intolerance or slower digestion, which becomes more common after the age of 50, this combination often leads to nausea, cramping, or rapid onset acid reflux. This pairing can also overload the liver. Beet juice increases nitric oxide activity and circulation, while milk slows digestion and thickens stomach contents. When consumed together, the liver must work harder to process metabolic byproducts from both foods simultaneously, increasing inflammation rather than reducing it. For those with fatty liver, sluggish digestion, or a history of reflux, this combination should be avoided entirely. If you want a richer beet-based drink, there are safer alternatives. Keep these foods separate, and your stomach, liver, and energy levels will benefit. Mistake number six is mixing beet juice with spinach. Individually, beetroot and spinach are nutrient-dense foods, but when combined in the same juice, especially in concentrated raw form, they create a biochemical burden that the kidneys and bones do not tolerate well. Both beetroot and spinach are extremely high in oxalates, natural plant compounds that bind to minerals such as calcium, magnesium, and iron. When consumed in large amounts or combined together, oxalates can overwhelm the body's ability to process and eliminate them. The greatest risk is kidney stones. Oxalates from both foods bind with calcium in the kidneys and form calcium oxalate crystals, the most common and painful type of kidney stone. This risk is especially high in people with a history of stones, reduced kidney function, mild dehydration, or low magnesium levels. This combination can quickly push the body into a high oxalate load. You may not feel it immediately, but crystals silently accumulate, increasing risk week after week. Over time, this can weaken bones, worsen anemia, or contribute to fatigue and muscle weakness. Individuals with gut conditions such as IBS, gastritis, or low stomach acid are particularly vulnerable. High oxilate juices pass through the digestive tract too quickly, irritating the intestines and causing symptoms such as bloating, cramping, loose stools, or sudden urgency. Older adults with reduced clearance of crystal forming compounds face an even higher risk of oxalate accumulation. This does not mean spinach is harmful or that beetroot should be avoided. It simply means they should not be juiced together, especially in large quantities. If you enjoy greens with beet juice, choose lower oxalate options such as kale, mint, celery leaves, or romaine. If you enjoy spinach, cook it or consume it separately at another time of day, not raw and combined with beetroot. Your kidneys work continuously to protect you. And this is a combination they should not be forced to handle. Mistake number seven is mixing beet juice with lemon. One of the most common habits people have is adding a large squeeze of lemon to their beet juice. It sounds healthy. Vitamin C, freshness, detoxification. So, what could go wrong? Quite a lot. Lemon is extremely acidic. And when that strong acidity interacts with the natural compounds in raw beetroot, the pH of the juice shifts immediately. For many older adults, especially those with gastritis, acid reflux, or sensitive stomachs, this can cause irritation within minutes. People often report heartburn, nausea, or stomach sourness shortly after drinking this combination without realizing that lemon is the trigger. But the larger issue is what happens to beetroot nutrients. Beetroot is naturally rich in plant-based iron and beneficial pigments that support blood formation and circulation. These nutrients require a stable internal environment to be absorbed properly. When lemon excessively acidifies the mixture, it disrupts that process. Instead of enhancing absorption, the acidity interferes with it, particularly iron absorption, effectively cancelling one of the main benefits people seek from beet juice. And if you are dealing with acid reflux, this combination can further irritate the esophagus. Lemon itself is not the villain. The problem lies in the quantity. A small hint of flavor may be harmless, but a large squeeze, half a lemon, or the juice of a whole lemon is where the trouble begins. Stay with me because the next benefits are essential. First, respect the dosage. The key number is 100 to 120 milliliters. The biggest mistake men make is believing that more is better. One medium-sized beet is the maximum daily limit. From a medical standpoint, the body can only process a limited amount of nitrate at one time. Drinking more does not increase vasoddilation. Instead, it raises oxalate levels, places strain on the kidneys, and can lead to buria, a sign that the body is overloaded and unable to process excess pigments. 100 to 120 ml of pure juice is the ideal range to support energy without overwhelming your filtration systems. Second, keep the juice clean and original. Do not add sugar, honey, or any sweeteners. As explained earlier, sugar is an enemy of nitric oxide. If the earthy taste feels too strong, dilute with cucumber or celery. These vegetables are rich in electrolytes and contain enzymes that support liver detoxification without raising blood sugar levels. Allow your taste to adapt to the natural flavor. This is a form of respect for your body. Third, the golden timing is 30 minutes before breakfast. Why? On an empty stomach. When the stomach is light, the conversion of nitrate from the mouth to the stomach is most effective. Beneficial oral bacteria need time to interact with the juice before it reaches the digestive tract. Do not rinse your mouth or brush your teeth immediately afterward. Allow the compounds to absorb naturally. A glass of beet juice early in the morning prepares your blood vessels for optimal dilation, helping you maintain alertness and endurance throughout the day. Fourth, drink slowly and treat it like a fine wine. Beet juice is not meant to be taken as a quick shot. Sip it slowly in small amounts. This allows enzymes in the saliva to begin nitrate conversion directly in the oral cavity. For older adults, slow consumption helps the blood pressure system adjust gradually to vasoddilation. Reducing the risk of dizziness from sudden drops in pressure. Fifth, follow the rotation rule to give the kidneys rest. Do not drink beet juice every day of the year. The body needs breaks to process accumulated oxalates. The best approach is five consecutive days followed by two days off or rotating with celery juice or cucumber water. This variety protects bone minerals and significantly reduces the risk of kidney stone formation. As you can see, health does not come from expensive miracles. It comes from understanding and discipline in the smallest habits. Preparing a glass of juice correctly is a way of showing respect for the remarkable biological system within you. The final question I want to leave you with tonight is this. Will you continue drinking based on habit and face silent risks? Or will you begin applying medical logic to truly take control of your health starting tomorrow? Remember, at the age of 60, the body no longer tolerates repeated mistakes. A small change in your juice preparation may be the beginning of a new and more vital chapter of your life. If this video helped you recognize a mistake you may have made, press like to share this message with the men around you. Share it with a friend who drinks juice daily. You may be helping them avoid a serious kidney issue. Thank you for listening. Stay mentally sharp and choose vitality every day. Goodbye and I will see you again

contract potassium is an electrolyte which means it dissolves in water electrolytes are important for the body to function properly because they help transmit electrical signals between nerve cells and muscle cells electrolytes also help regulate blood pressure by allowing your body to retain water or release it into the bloodstream as needed beets contain about 400 milligrams of potassium per choline in the body choline is an essential nutrient that helps nerves send messages from one location to another within the body without enough choline nerve cells cannot function properly and flow throughout your body including your muscles and brain this allows for greater oxygen delivery to your organs and muscles so you can perform better during workouts but tannin and beets is also news is that many foods can help lower blood pressure naturally if you have high blood pressure you may want to give beet juice a try a new study found that drinking beet juice can significantly lower your blood pressure beets are packed with nutrients like folate potassium and magnesium these minerals help to relax blood vessels and reduce inflammation which are both common cause of high blood pressure beet juice also increases nitric oxide levels in your body which relaxes your blood vessels and lowers your blood pressure a study found that after two weeks of drinking beet juice the participants had a significant reduction in their systolic blood pressure systolic blood pressure is the top number that represents the force of blood against artery walls when the heart beats number videos.title: 9 POWERFUL Beetroot & Ginger Juice Health Benefits You Need to Know | How To Make it

transcript.fullText: the beautiful bright color of beetroot can make anyone fall in love with them yet they're ignored perhaps because of their distinctive taste if you're not a fan you might want to think of an easier way to still enjoy the many health benefits of beetroot without having to actually eat it meat roots are best enjoyed raw and uncooked and juicing them up is a quick and simple method of consuming them add ginger to the mix which is known for being one of the most powerful natural medicines in the world and you're on to a winner here's everything you need to know about the health benefits of beetroot and ginger juice one it manages your blood pressure one of the benefits of beetroot juice and ginger is that it can help in temporarily lowering high blood pressure one reason for this is the presence of naturally occurring nitrates in beets which increase nitric oxide in the blood vessels allowing more oxygen to flow to your brain heart and muscles the anti-inflammatory compounds in ginger can also help manage blood pressure one study review found that ginger can lower blood pressure and decrease blood fat levels both of which help protect against heart disease two it gives you glowing skin if you're healthy from within it reflects on the outside beetroot and ginger juice act as a great blood purifier which is key in keeping your skin glowing and healthy meat roots are also rich in vitamin c which helps in clearing blemishes and evens out your skin tone while giving in a natural glow three it's a great way to detox naturally beet roots are a unique source of phytonutrients these compounds are known for their powerful antioxidant anti-inflammatory and detoxification properties for this reason beetroot juice in particular is a great liver cleanser and helps to flush out toxins ginger has also proved an effective detoxing food with some studies linking it to benefits and weight loss and there's definitely some promising research linking ginger to weight management 4. boost energy and stamina beetroot and ginger juice helps to open the blood vessels and increase the flow of oxygen throughout your body making you feel more energetic and active that's why so many people prefer to drink this juice in the morning so they can enjoy an energized start to the day it has often been promoted as a great workout drink and good for athletes thanks to its ability to increase oxygen to the muscles preparing them for the stress ahead 5. good for digestion beetroot and ginger juice is full of fiber which helps to regulate digestive processes and relieve constipation beetroot is also beneficial for good digestive health it's thought that beetroot increases stomach acid levels a glass of beetroot and ginger juice is also a great home remedy for an upset stomach research has linked multiple digestive benefits to ginger specifically feelings of nausea stomach upset and vomiting it may also help move food from the stomach to the small intestine for digestion and absorption 6. lowers blood glucose diabetes is a huge problem around the world and while drinking beetroot and ginger juice is far from a diabetes cure it may help someone in preventing and managing it research has linked some active compounds in ginger with improvements in insulin and metabolism and though high in sugar beetroot actually helps in regulating your blood sugar levels studies have shown that it doesn't lead to high glucose concentration in the blood the natural sugars in beet root are released very slowly in the body preventing sudden spikes 7. it's an anti-inflammatory like other produce ginger and beets contain antioxidant-like compounds that may reduce cell damage the root of ginger can also prevent inflammation by reducing cell signaling activity another reason to drink the two together eight can curb morning sickness pregnant women take note ginger and beetroot juice may help reduce symptoms of morning sickness research has found that it's safe to take ginger during pregnancy and it has shown improvement in morning sickness symptoms when compared to a placebo nine may lower your risk of cancer the cell protecting properties of ginger can lower the long-term risk of certain cancers that's because the spice and other flavorings can reduce cellular activity that causes dna changes cell death and proliferation of cancer cells so how can you make beetroot and ginger juice well it's quick and easy though you will need a juicer or a blender for the job start by washing and scrubbing your beets and ginger peel and cut the beets and ginger into small pieces that you can feed into the juicer many people also choose to squeeze a little lemon juice but that's completely up to you stir well and drink up if you only have a blender don't panic you can still make the juice with it just remove the peels from your ingredients blend until smooth and then strain through a nut milk bag or fine mesh sieve squeezing to get out as much juice as possible if you don't currently start your day with beetroot and ginger juice what are you waiting for this simple concoction is incredibly helpful so it's definitely worth adding to your diet sooner rather than later i hope you've enjoyed this video if you have please subscribe to this youtube channel and don't forget to give this video the thumbs up and i'm sure you're going to enjoy this next video on six health benefits of baking soda and apple cider vinegar tonic drink thanks for watching and bye for now you
